class.name: FmodReverb3D
class.brief_description: Represents a 3D reverb zone for spatial audio reverberation effects.
class.description: FmodReverb3D creates a spherical reverb zone in 3D space that applies environmental reverberation to sounds based on their distance from the reverb center. This allows you to create realistic spatial audio environments where sounds become more reverberant as they enter specific areas (like caves, halls, or rooms). Key features: - Spherical reverb zone with configurable min/max distance - Full control over reverb parameters (decay time, delays, diffusion, density) - High and low frequency control for realistic room modeling - Smooth transition as sounds enter/exit the reverb zone When a sound source is within the min_distance, it receives full reverb. Between min and max distance, the reverb amount fades based on the rolloff curve. Beyond max_distance, no reverb is applied. [b]Note:[/b] Use [method FmodSystem.create_reverb_3d] to create instances of this class.

method.name: get_3d_attributes
method.description: Returns the 3D attributes of this reverb zone as a Dictionary: - [code]position[/code]: Position of the reverb center (Vector3) - [code]min_distance[/code]: Minimum distance for full reverb effect (float) - [code]max_distance[/code]: Maximum distance where reverb fades to zero (float)

method.name: get_active
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if this reverb zone is active and processing audio. Inactive reverb zones do not contribute to the audio output.

method.name: get_decay_time
method.description: Returns the reverberation decay time in milliseconds. This is the time it takes for the reverb to decay by 60dB.

method.name: get_density
method.description: Returns the modal density value (0.0 to 100.0). Controls the coloration of the late reverb decay. Higher values result in more metallic ringing.

method.name: get_diffusion
method.description: Returns the echo density value (0.0 to 100.0). Controls the density of echoes in the late reverb decay. Higher values create a smoother, more dense reverb tail.

method.name: get_early_delay
method.description: Returns the initial reflection delay time in milliseconds. This is the time between the direct sound and the first reflections.

method.name: get_early_late_mix
method.description: Returns the early reflections level relative to the room effect in dB. Controls the balance between early reflections and the late reverb tail.

method.name: get_hf_decay_ratio
method.description: Returns the high frequency decay ratio (0.0 to 100.0). Percentage of the high frequency decay time relative to the mid-frequency decay time. Lower values simulate air absorption in large spaces.

method.name: get_hf_reference
method.description: Returns the reference high frequency in Hz. Frequencies above this value are affected by the HF decay ratio.

method.name: get_high_cut
method.description: Returns the high frequency cutoff relative to the room effect in dB. Controls the level of high frequency reflections.

method.name: get_late_delay
method.description: Returns the late reverb delay time in milliseconds. This is the time between the early reflections and the onset of the late reverb tail.

method.name: get_low_shelf_frequency
method.description: Returns the reference low frequency in Hz. Frequencies below this value are affected by the low shelf gain.

method.name: get_low_shelf_gain
method.description: Returns the low shelf gain in dB. Controls the relative room effect level for low frequencies.

method.name: get_wet_level
method.description: Returns the wet signal level in dB. Controls the overall reverb output level relative to the dry signal.

method.name: reverb_3d_is_null
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the reverb handle is null or invalid. A null reverb indicates that the object has not been properly initialized or has been released.

method.name: reverb_3d_is_valid
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the reverb handle is valid and ready for use. A valid reverb can have its parameters modified and will contribute to the audio output.

method.name: release
method.description: Releases this reverb object and frees its memory. After calling this method, the reverb becomes invalid and should not be used.

method.name: set_3d_attributes
method.description: Sets the 3D attributes of the reverb zone. - [param position]: The center position of the reverb sphere in world space - [param min_distance]: Distance from center where reverb is at full strength - [param max_distance]: Distance from center where reverb fades to zero [b]Note:[/b] min_distance should be less than max_distance for proper fading behavior.

method.name: set_active
method.description: Enables or disables this reverb zone. When inactive ([code]false[/code]), the reverb does not contribute to the audio output. Use this to toggle reverb on/off without destroying and recreating it.

method.name: set_decay_time
method.description: Sets the reverberation decay time in milliseconds (range: 0.0 to 20000.0). This controls how long the reverb tail lasts. Typical values: - Small room: 200-800 ms - Large room: 800-1500 ms - Hall/Cave: 1500-3000 ms

method.name: set_density
method.description: Sets the modal density (range: 0.0 to 100.0, default: 100.0). Controls the coloration of the late reverb. Lower values create more distinct echoes, higher values create a smoother decay.

method.name: set_diffusion
method.description: Sets the echo density (range: 0.0 to 100.0, default: 50.0). Controls how dense the late reverb tail sounds. Higher values sound smoother and more natural.

method.name: set_early_delay
method.description: Sets the initial reflection delay in milliseconds (range: 0.0 to 300.0). This is the time before the first reflections arrive. Larger rooms have longer early delays.

method.name: set_early_late_mix
method.description: Sets the early reflections level in dB (range: -100.0 to 100.0, default: 50.0). Controls the balance between early reflections and the late reverb tail.

method.name: set_hf_decay_ratio
method.description: Sets the high frequency decay ratio in percent (range: 10.0 to 100.0, default: 50.0). Controls how quickly high frequencies decay compared to mid frequencies. Lower values simulate air absorption in large spaces or soft materials.

method.name: set_hf_reference
method.description: Sets the reference high frequency in Hz (range: 20.0 to 20000.0, default: 5000.0). Frequencies above this value are affected by the HF decay ratio setting.

method.name: set_high_cut
method.description: Sets the high frequency cutoff in dB (range: -100.0 to 0.0, default: 0.0). Attenuates high frequency reflections to simulate soft materials or air absorption.

method.name: set_late_delay
method.description: Sets the late reverb delay in milliseconds (range: 0.0 to 100.0, default: 11.0). This is the time between early reflections and the onset of the late reverb tail.

method.name: set_low_shelf_frequency
method.description: Sets the reference low frequency in Hz (range: 20.0 to 1000.0, default: 250.0). Frequencies below this value are affected by the low shelf gain setting.

method.name: set_low_shelf_gain
method.description: Sets the low shelf gain in dB (range: -100.0 to 100.0, default: 0.0). Boosts or cuts low frequency reverb to simulate room size and materials. Positive values simulate larger rooms or hard surfaces.

method.name: set_wet_level
method.description: Sets the wet (reverb) signal level in dB (range: -100.0 to 20.0, default: -6.0). Controls the overall loudness of the reverb effect.

members.member: Whether this reverb zone is active and contributing to the audio output.
Modal density of the late reverb (0.0 to 100.0).
Echo density of the late reverb (0.0 to 100.0).
Initial reflection delay time in milliseconds.
Early reflections level relative to room effect.
High frequency decay ratio as percentage.
Late reverb delay time in milliseconds.
